article_title: Planning and Development Committee Meeting — July 06, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Low-rise multi-family development with childcare at 3588 Wayburne Drive
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to consider a rezoning application (REZ #25-16) for 3588 Wayburne Drive. The proposal would change the zoning to allow a low-rise multi-family residential building that includes a childcare facility. Council will vote on whether to send this application to 'First and Second Reading' — which are the first two steps in the formal approval process for a new zoning bylaw (a local law that controls how land can be used). This is not a final decision yet; it is a procedural step to move the proposal forward for further review and public input.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — this is your chance to comment on the proposed rezoning.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
6.1
REZ #25-16 - 3588 WAYBURNE DRIVE - AMENDED LOW-RISE MULTI-FAMILY DEVELOPMENT WITH CHILDCARE FACILITY
Purpose: To seek Council authorization to forward REZ #25-16 to First and Second Reading at a future Council meeting.
agenda_briefs.article_title: Planning and Development Committee Meeting Agenda — July 06, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Six-storey market rental building at 5168-5192 Sidley Street
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on whether to move forward with a rezoning application for a six-storey market rental building at 5168-5192 Sidley Street, in the Royal Oak area. The proposal would allow a new rental housing development. Council will consider sending the application to a first and second reading — these are early steps in the approval process, not a final decision. A public hearing would come later if the proposal advances.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — this is your chance to comment on the proposed rental building before Council makes a final decision.

key_dates.label: Council vote on first and second reading — proposed Sidley Street rezoning

agenda_briefs.source_body:
6.2
REZ #26-07 - 5168, 5180 AND 5192 SIDLEY STREET – SIX-STOREY MARKET RENTAL (ROYAL OAK COMMUNITY PLAN)
Purpose: To seek Council authorization to forward REZ #26-07 to First and Second Reading at a future Council meeting.
agenda_briefs.article_title: Planning and Development Committee Meeting Agenda — July 06, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to review progress on HOME housing strategy and city land projects
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will receive a progress update on the HOME: Burnaby’s Housing and Homelessness Strategy and on non-market housing projects being built on city-owned land. This is a report on what has been done so far, not a final decision. The update covers actions taken under the strategy and the status of city-led affordable housing developments. No new rules or approvals are being voted on at this time.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.1
PROGRESS UPDATE: 2025 HOME STRATEGY IMPLEMENTATION AND CITY NON-MARKET HOUSING PROJECTS
Purpose: To provide a progress update on actions under HOME: Burnaby’s Housing and Homelessness Strategy and the non-market housing projects on City-owned lands.
agenda_briefs.article_title: Planning and Development Committee Meeting Agenda — July 06, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on Rathburn Drive student housing rezoning
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposal (REZ #25-19) to rezone land on Rathburn Drive for student housing. The proposal is currently under review, and a public hearing may be scheduled. This decision could affect property owners, developers, and landlords in the area by changing what can be built on the site. The item was referred from the May 12, 2026, Council Committee of the Whole (CCP) meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — attend or submit written comments to have your say on this rezoning proposal.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.1
Tim Ramage - REZ #25-19 - Rathburn Drive Student Housing Proposal
(Referred from CCP May 12, 2026)
agenda_briefs.article_title: Planning and Development Committee Meeting Agenda — July 06, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Rathburn Drive student housing development in Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to consider a proposed student housing development on Rathburn Drive. The proposal is described in correspondence from Veda and Larry Yee, which was referred from a Community Planning Committee meeting on May 12, 2026. This is not yet approved — Council will vote on whether to move forward with the project. If approved, the development would create new housing specifically for students in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the upcoming council vote on this proposal — if you live or own a business near Rathburn Drive, consider attending the public hearing to share your views.

key_dates.label: Proposed Rathburn Drive student housing — council vote date TBD

agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.2
Veda and Larry Yee - Proposed Rathburn Drive Student Housing Development
(Referred from CCP May 12, 2026)
agenda_briefs.article_title: Planning and Development Committee Meeting Agenda — July 06, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Axin Architecture seeks change to Burnaby daycare application policy
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a request from Axin Architecture Ltd. to change the policy for daycare applications in Burnaby. This item was referred from the May 12, 2026 Community and Corporate Planning (CCP) committee meeting. The proposed change has not yet been approved — Council is set to vote on whether to adopt the new policy at an upcoming meeting. If approved, the change could affect how daycare operators and developers apply for permits.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Contact your Burnaby councillor before the vote to share how this policy change would affect your business.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.3
Axin Architecture Ltd. - Request to Change the Policy for Daycare Application
(Referred from CCP May 12, 2026)
